(o 4) [...] will act effectively.

(o 5) [...] will experience distress.

(o 6) [...] will not acquire [...].

(o 7) [...] will seize [...].

(o 9) [...] ... will govern his land.

o 1010

[...] x TAB? DUMU LUGAL AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat

(o 10) [...] ... the king's son will seize his father's throne.

o 1111

[...] AN.TA-MEŠ la₃! GAL₂-MEŠ KUR ⸢NUN⸣ ana ⸢KISLAḪ₃!⸣ NIGIN⸢ḫur!(.IM)

(o 11) [...] there are no upper [...]: the prince's land will gather into a fortress.

o 1212

[...] KI.TA-MEŠ la₃ GAL₂ši x ŠU₂ U₄ DAB [(x)]

(o 12) [...] there are no lower [...]: he will seize ... [...].

o 1313

x UR₂ SU.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂-⸢ma⸣ gišTUKUL NUN KI.GUB-šu NU GURra

(o 13) [...] ...: there will be a famine in the land and the prince's weapon will not return to its (rightful) place.

o 1414

[...] taq₂-ti-it BAL KUR ana KISLAḪ₃ NIGIN₂ur₂

(o 14) [...] end of the reign; the land will gather into a fortress.

o 1515

[...]-ma DUMU-šu₂ AŠ.TE DAB-ma ana AD-šu₂ u₂-wa-tar

(o 15) [...] and his son will seize the throne and will surpass his father.

o 1616

[...] x NUN ina KUR-šu₂ ZAḪ₂

(o 16) [...] the prince will disappear from his land.

o 1717

[...] ILLU ina IDIM uḫ-ḫa-ra EREN₂ NUN TUR KUR₂ IRI EN.NUN-ia₂ ina me-ku-tu₂ DABbat

(o 17) [...] the high waters will be delayed in the sources; the prince's troops will decrease; the enemy will take over my garrison city through shortage.

o 1818

[...] x ⸢i⸣-ḫe-er-ri ILLU ina IDIM DUkam KUR NUN DAGAL IRI EN.NUN KUR₂ ina me-ku-tu₂ ta-ṣa-bat

(o 18) [...] he will dig; the high water will flow from the sources; the prince's land will increase; you will take over the enemy's garrison city through shortage.

o 1919

[...] taq₂-ti-it BAL SU.GU₇ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 19) [...] end of reign; there will be famine in the land.

o 2020

[...] SU.⸢GU₇⸣ ina KUR KUR₂ GAL₂ši

(o 20) [...] there will be famine in the enemy's land.

o 2121

[...] TUR₃ LU₂ TURer

(o 21) [...] the man's animal stall will reduce.

o 2222

[...] TUR₃ EN DU₁₁-ka TURer

(o 22) [...] your enemy's animal stall will reduce.

o 2323

[...] KI.ḪUL ina KUR NUN ŠUBdi

(o 23) [...] a mourning rite will be imposed on the prince's land.

o 2424

[...] KI.ḪUL ina KUR KUR₂ ŠUBdi

(o 24) [...] a mourning rite will be imposed on the enemy's land.

o 2525

[...] EREN₂-ka ana ni-iš i-ni-ka DU.DU

(o 25) [...] the troops will keep ranging at your discretion.

o 2626

[...] ZI.GA dan-nu ana KUR ZI.GA

(o 26) [...] a massive attack will arise against the land.

o 2727

[...] LU₂ UGU EN DU₁₁-šu₂ GUBaz

(o 27) [...] the man will triumph over his enemy.

o 2828

[...] ZIib MUNUS.ḪUL ana KUR NUN ZI-am

(o 28) [...] an onset of evil will arise against the prince's land.

o 2929

[...] ⸢DUMU⸣ LUGAL AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat

(o 29) [...] the king's son will seize his father's throne.

o 3030

[...] ⸢DUMU⸣ LUGAL ša₂ KUR KUR₂ AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat

(o 30) [...] the king's son of the enemy's land will seize his father's throne.

o 3131

[...] ⸢KUR⸣ NUN TUM₃

(o 31) [...] the prince's land will disappear.

o 3232

[...] ⸢KUR₂⸣-šu₂ TUM₃

(o 32) [...] of his enemy will disappear.

o 3333

[...] TUR₃ BI BIRaḫ

(o 33) [...] that animal stall will be scattered.

o 3434

[...] ⸢TUR₃⸣ BI DAGALiš ⸢TUR₃⸣ KUR₂ BIRaḫ

(o 34) [...] that animal stall will expand; the enemy's animal stall will be scattered.

(o 35) [...] completion.

[...] x KUR TIqe₂

(o 36) [...] he will take the land.

(r 15') [...] ....

(r 23') [...] will put [...].

(r 25') [...] he will seize the throne and will live long.

(r 32') [...] he will revolt (against) his [...].

r 33'33'

[...] ⸢AŠ⸣.TE DABbat

(r 33') [...] he will seize the throne.

r 34'34'

[...] x dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) KUR GU₇

(r 34') [...] Nergal will devour his land.

r 35'35'

[...] ⸢BAL⸣ dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) ka-šu₂-šu₂ ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 35') [...] reign of Nergal; there will be a catastrophe in the land.

r 36'36'

[...] SU ZALAG₂ir EGIR-šu₂ KUR-su BIRaḫ

(r 36') [...] he will beam; after him, his land will be scattered.

r 37'37'

[...] ⸢un⸣-na-aš-ma EGIR-šu₂ KUR-su SI.SA₂

(r 37') [...] he will weaken the [...] and after him, his land will thrive.

r 38'38'

[...] KUR LUGAL EN TI.LA NU SI.SA₂

(r 38') [...] the king's land will not thrive until he is healthy.

r 39'39'

[...] TU TU KUR-su BIRaḫ

(r 39') [...] ... his land will be scattered.

r 40'40'

[...] ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 40') [...] there will be [...] in the land.

r 41'41'

[...] ina KUR KUR₂ GAL₂ši

(r 41') [...] there will be [...] in the enemy's land.

r 42'42'

[... ina] gišTUKUL GAZ-šu₂-ma IRI u na-me-e-šu₂ TUM₃

(r 42') [...] he will kill him with a weapon and will take away the city and its pasturage.

r 43'43'

[...] ŠEŠ LUGAL ana KUR₂-šu₂ GUN IL₂ši

(r 43') [...] the king's brother will carry tribute to his enemy.

r 44'44'

[...] KUR₂-šu₂ ina gišTUKUL GAZ-ma IRI u na-me-e-šu₂ TUM₃

(r 44') [...] his enemy will kill (him) with a weapon and will take away the city and its pasturage.

r 45'45'

[...] DUMU KUR₂ ana LUGAL GUN IL₂ši

(r 45') [...] the enemy's son will carry tribute to the king.

r 46'46'

[...] NU GAR-MEŠ IRI BI ZAḪ₂-ma NIG₂.BA-šu₂ SIG₅ KUR₂ GU₇

(r 46') [...] they will put [...]; that city will be destroyed and the enemy will devastate its beautiful gift(s).

r 47'47'

[...] TA NU GAR-MEŠ IRI KUR₂ ZAḪ₂-ma NIG₂.BA-šu₂ SIG₅ ta-kal

(r 47') [...] they will put [...]; the enemy's city will be destroyed; you will devastate its beautiful gift(s).

r 48'48'

[...] GIR₃ ŠAḪ IRI ZA₃-ka KUR₂ BALkat₂

(r 48') [...] pig's foot: your border city will defect (to) the enemy.

r 49'49'

[...] GIR₃ UDU.NITA₂ IRI ZA₃ ⸢KUR₂ BAL⸣-ka

(r 49') [...] ram's foot: the enemy's border city will defect (to) you.

r 50'50'

[...] ŠA₂ ŠU₂ x x x [...]-MEŠ ša₂ KUR ENel

(r 50') [...] ... he will rull over the [...] of the land.

(catchline)
r 51'51'

[...] ŠA₃ ER₂

(r 51') [...] "... weeps".

(colophon)
r 52'52'

[...] IM m30—30? lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ BAN₃.DA

(r 52') [...] Tablet of ..., junior incantation priest; [...] hand of Bel-ah-iddin [...] son of Ekur-zakir.

r 53'53'

[...] qa-at₂ mdEN—ŠEŠ—MU

r 54'54'

[...] x EŠ ḪA DUMU mE₂.KUR—za-kir
